description_long: This object is a combat-style knife featuring a long, straight, double-edged blade with a sharp point, designed for thrusting and cutting. The blade is metallic and appears to be made of steel, with a smooth, slightly reflective finish. The handle is distinctive, constructed from a bronze or brass-colored metal and shaped like a set of knuckle dusters, providing both grip and potential for striking. There is a small, flat guard separating the blade from the handle, enhancing safety during use. The overall design is utilitarian with a focus on durability and functionality, lacking any visible decorative elements. The object is compact and robust, suitable for close-quarters use in urban or survival scenarios.
description: A combat knife with a straight, double-edged steel blade and a brass knuckle-style handle, separated by a small guard. The design is utilitarian and robust, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight blade and the brass knuckle-style handle with four finger holes.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the width of the handle, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's length and the handle's finger holes, similar to view 0 but reversed.
description_view_3: Edge-on view from the opposite side, again emphasizing the slim profile of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: brass knuckle-style, metallic, four finger holes, straight, solid grip
